_cascade_disclosure: D4 RETROFIT — v2.0.0 building_diversity + cable-sizing cascade additions. DEFERRED-POINTER state: consumed_intents.cable_sizing.source_path points at electrical/cable-sizing/examples/intl-open-plan-floor-feeder/intent-out.json — a producer-side fixture that does NOT yet exist at C.7-ship (D4 sprint). Payload bytes are INLINED byte-identical with the small-power IR diversified_max_load_a values. When the producer fixture lands, source_path will resolve to a real file but the payload bytes are expected to remain unchanged so INV-19 PASS does not regress. This example was originally authored at v1.0.0 (2026-05-19) as a leaf skill (no cascades). The D4 retrofit is purely ADDITIVE — same 8 circuits, 9 rooms, 8 sockets, same supply, same jurisdiction (INT). Sections 1-8 of reasoning.md remain valid for the v2.0 IR; section 9 documents the retrofit additions.
site_brief: 350 m² commercial open-plan office floor. 400V TPN+E supply per IEC 60364-4-41:2017. 24 workstation positions + 2 meeting rooms + kitchenette + server cabinet area + toilet block + outdoor sockets. Server cabinet small-power is UPS-fed with Type B 30mA RCD per IEC 60364-5-53:2002+A2:2015 §531.3.3 (matches db-layout intl-dbcomms-data shipped policy for IT equipment with DC leakage components).

building_diversity_inputs._d4_retrofit_note: v2.0.0 D4 RETROFIT (added 2026-06-03). This input block was NOT present in the v1.0.0 ship of this example. Honest disclosure: pre-D4 there was no inputs.json taxonomy item for building_diversity_inputs at all — added in D4 A.2 alongside the IR schema additions in A.1.
building_diversity_inputs._profile_reference_note: IEC 60364 does not publish a dedicated diversity-factor table in any verified part (60364-3 assessment of general characteristics covers maximum demand at a principle level only). For INT jurisdiction the engineer-of-record applies the IET On-Site Guide 8th Edition Appendix A Table A1 office_small_power_workstation profile as the best-available working reference — this is widely used internationally for IEC 60364 designs because IEC 60364-1:2005 §132.12 (design margin) and IEC 60364-3 §31 (maximum demand) leave the factor selection to engineering judgement. The IET OSG values are cited here HONESTLY as a cross-jurisdiction reference, not as a binding IEC norm.
